title: Heat Inactivate FBS
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"aqqmh","text":"Heat inactivate FBS to denature compliment. Critical for trypanosomes but not for EA.hy 926 cells. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Allow serum to acclimate at room temperature for a minimum of 10 minutes or refrigerate overnight at 2° C to 8° C.
The serum may then be completely thawed at 37 °C in a water bath. Swirl the bottles throughout the thawing process every 10-15 minutes as this will disperse the released salts and proteins uniformly.

Prepare a water bath, if not already done so during the thawing process, that can be temperature controlled to 56 °C in for the heat inactivation process. There must be enough water to submerge the entire contents of all bottles.
In cell culture hood, aliquot 50 mls into 10 50 ml containers.
Remove the heat inactivated serum after the 30 minutes and gently swirl once again.
Gently swirl the bottles every 3-5 minutes to make certain the serum remains uniform throughout heating.
Place the serum into the 56 °C in water bath for 00:30:00
Inactivating serum that has not been thoroughly mixed homogeneously
Inactivating serum above the recommended temperature
Inactivating serum for a longer period of time than recommended 30 minutes
